reasoning: The phrase "snack foods" is explicitly on the USDA "What Can SNAP Buy?" page as of May 20, 2025. While the Trump administration (USDA Sec. Brooke Rollins and HHS Sec. RFK Jr.) has announced its intent to ban "junk food" and "sugary drinks" from SNAP, actually changing the allowed items requires either an act of Congress or a lengthy state-by-state waiver process. There is no evidence that this policy change will be finalized before the July 1, 2025, resolution date (only 6 weeks away). Furthermore, while the administration could theoretically scrub the website prematurely, there are no reports of them doing so over the past 4 months, and doing so while the foods remain legally eligible would only cause administrative confusion. Government website text has high inertia, leading to a high probability that the text remains through the 6-week window.
